input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

compressed_segments.text:
[0-4] Text "Arvind G" shifts from a diagonal slant to a horizontal position at the top, completing to "Arvind Gupta Toys". A central geometric hexagon diagram featuring red nodes connected by thick black and thinner grey lines appears centrally. Below this graphic, the caption changes from "Hands-0" to "Hands-on Science" in black font.
[4-8] The scene cuts to a blue background featuring a toy car constructed from a clear plastic bottle with button wheels. A red balloon is attached via a green straw. A hand rests on the left side of the car. Yellow text "TOYS FROM TRASH" and green text "JET CAR" appear. A subtitle "Dubbed in French by Louis Jourdin (Les petits derovillards)" is added before fading to black.
[8-12] A blue background appears displaying the yellow title "MATERIALS". Below, labeled components are arranged: a small plastic bottle with button wheels and a needle, a red balloon, a rubber band, and a green bent straw. White text labels clearly identify each item: "NEEDLE", "BIG BUTTONS", "SMALL PLASTIC BOTTLE", "BALLOON", "RUBBER BAND", and "BENT STRAW" against the blue surface. The static display persists.

[14-18] A hand holds a clear plastic bottle car with button wheels and a red balloon on a green straw, captioned "BOTTLE JET CAR". The car is tilted upwards to show the underside and the red balloon located inside the bottle.
[18-22] The yellow text changes to "BUTTON WHEELS + NEEDLE AXLE + STRAW BUSH" as the hand maintains the tilt, revealing button wheels mounted on needle axles with straw segments acting as bushings.
[22-26] The caption updates to "BENT STRAW + BALLOON" while the hand rotates the vehicle, displaying the internal green straw connected to the red balloon against the blue background.

[26-30] Against a blue background, a hand displays a DIY car made from a clear plastic bottle with wooden button wheels on needle axles. A red balloon is attached to a green straw inside. The text "BENT STRAW + BALLOON" is visible as the car rotates from an underside view to a side profile.
[30-32] The background changes to a grey textured wall. A man with dark hair and a mustache holds the bottle car near his face. The yellow text updates to "INFLATE BALLOON & CLOSE STRAW".
[32-38] The man blows into the green straw, inflating the red balloon. It expands from a small shape into a large, spherical balloon attached to the top of the vehicle. He holds the clear bottle steady with puffed cheeks as the balloon reaches full inflation.

recent_thinks: [12-14] The static materials display cuts to a hand holding the assembled bottle car against a blue background. The red balloon is attached to the green straw mounted on the bottle's top. Yellow text reading "BOTTLE JET CAR" appears in the lower left corner, labeling the completed toy vehicle with button wheels.

[38-40] The man ceases blowing and pinches the green straw shut with his fingers to trap air inside the fully inflated red balloon. He holds the clear plastic bottle car steady against the grey wall while the yellow caption "INFLATE BALLOON & CLOSE STRAW" remains displayed at the bottom left corner.

[40-42] The man holds the clear bottle car with the large red balloon fully inflated against a grey wall. He pinches the green straw with his fingers to trap the air inside. The yellow instructional text overlay disappears from the bottom left corner as he maintains his grip on the stationary vehicle.

[42-44] The scene transitions to a grey floor where the bottle car rests with its red balloon inflated. A hand holds the vehicle steady on the surface. Yellow text appears in the lower left corner displaying the instruction "PLACE ON FLOOR & LET GO !!".

[44-46] A hand releases the clear plastic bottle car on a grey floor. Propelled by air escaping the red balloon, the vehicle rolls forward away from the camera. The yellow text "PLACE ON FLOOR & LET GO !!" remains overlaid in the bottom left corner as the car travels across the smooth surface.

[46-48] The clear bottle car travels further across the polished grey floor, moving away from the camera towards the background office furniture. As the red balloon deflates, propelling the vehicle, it becomes smaller in the frame. The yellow text "PLACE ON FLOOR & LET GO !!" stays visible in the bottom left corner throughout the motion.

queries.question: Which text label appears at the top of the screen when the craft materials are displayed? A. TOYS FROM TRASH B. JET CAR C. MATERIALS D. BOTTLE JET CAR

answers.text: C

samples.output: <think>The toy car rolls completely out of the frame or becomes indistinguishable in the distance, leaving the polished grey floor empty. The background office furniture remains visible as the vehicle disappears from view. The yellow text "PLACE ON FLOOR & LET GO !!" persists in the bottom left corner over the static scene of the room.</think><answer></answer>

compressed_segments.text:
[0-4] Text "Arvind G" shifts from a diagonal slant to a horizontal position at the top, completing to "Arvind Gupta Toys". A central geometric hexagon diagram featuring red nodes connected by thick black and thinner grey lines appears centrally. Below this graphic, the caption changes from "Hands-0" to "Hands-on Science" in black font.
[4-8] The scene cuts to a blue background featuring a toy car constructed from a clear plastic bottle with button wheels. A red balloon is attached via a green straw. A hand rests on the left side of the car. Yellow text "TOYS FROM TRASH" and green text "JET CAR" appear. A subtitle "Dubbed in French by Louis Jourdin (Les petits derovillards)" is added before fading to black.
[8-12] A blue background appears displaying the yellow title "MATERIALS". Below, labeled components are arranged: a small plastic bottle with button wheels and a needle, a red balloon, a rubber band, and a green bent straw. White text labels clearly identify each item: "NEEDLE", "BIG BUTTONS", "SMALL PLASTIC BOTTLE", "BALLOON", "RUBBER BAND", and "BENT STRAW" against the blue surface. The static display persists.

[14-18] A hand holds a clear plastic bottle car with button wheels and a red balloon on a green straw, captioned "BOTTLE JET CAR". The car is tilted upwards to show the underside and the red balloon located inside the bottle.
[18-22] The yellow text changes to "BUTTON WHEELS + NEEDLE AXLE + STRAW BUSH" as the hand maintains the tilt, revealing button wheels mounted on needle axles with straw segments acting as bushings.
[22-26] The caption updates to "BENT STRAW + BALLOON" while the hand rotates the vehicle, displaying the internal green straw connected to the red balloon against the blue background.

[26-30] Against a blue background, a hand displays a DIY car made from a clear plastic bottle with wooden button wheels on needle axles. A red balloon is attached to a green straw inside. The text "BENT STRAW + BALLOON" is visible as the car rotates from an underside view to a side profile.
[30-32] The background changes to a grey textured wall. A man with dark hair and a mustache holds the bottle car near his face. The yellow text updates to "INFLATE BALLOON & CLOSE STRAW".
[32-38] The man blows into the green straw, inflating the red balloon. It expands from a small shape into a large, spherical balloon attached to the top of the vehicle. He holds the clear bottle steady with puffed cheeks as the balloon reaches full inflation.

[38-42] A man holds a clear plastic bottle car with yellow button wheels and a fully inflated red balloon against a grey wall, pinching the green straw to trap air while "INFLATE BALLOON & CLOSE STRAW" is displayed. [42-46] The setting changes to a grey floor where a hand places the car down as the text updates to "PLACE ON FLOOR & LET GO !!". The hand releases the vehicle, and escaping air propels it forward. [46-50] The car rolls across the polished floor toward background office furniture as the red balloon deflates, eventually moving out of sight while the text remains on screen.

[50-54] A hand holds a clear plastic bottle car with an inflated red balloon, green straw, and button wheels stationary on a grey surface, then releases it. [54-60] The car propels forward across the polished floor, moving away from the camera past white partitions and a potted plant towards the far left near a white cabinet and brown chairs by a window. [60-62] The scene cuts to an end screen with text boxes "Click here to suggest activities for our future videos" and "Click here to subscribe and know more about our future videos". Thumbnails show "TOYS FROM TRASH D.C. MOTOR" and "TOYS FROM TRASH MAGNETIC LEVITATION". The footer displays "www.arvindguptatoys.com", social icons, and credits: "Ashok Rupner, Dr. Vidula Mhaiskar, Shivaji Mane, Jyoti Hiremath, Arvind Gupta, Manish Jain".

recent_thinks: [12-14] The static materials display cuts to a hand holding the assembled bottle car against a blue background. The red balloon is attached to the green straw mounted on the bottle's top. Yellow text reading "BOTTLE JET CAR" appears in the lower left corner, labeling the completed toy vehicle with button wheels.

[62-64] The end screen persists with top text boxes. The lower video thumbnails update to show hands interacting with the projects: a hand holds a copper coil over a battery on the left, and a hand holds a rod on a pink board on the right. Credits text appears at the bottom left above the website URL.

queries.question: Which text label appears at the top of the screen when the craft materials are displayed? A. TOYS FROM TRASH B. JET CAR C. MATERIALS D. BOTTLE JET CAR

answers.text: C
